title: Make Something Real with AI

modules.title: What Does Co-Creation Even Mean
modules.sub: Exploring what it really means to make something with AI
modules.description: Learners examine what changes when an AI is involved in a creative process and whether the result still counts as truly yours. They explore real examples of human-AI collaboration across music, art, and writing to challenge their assumptions. By the end, they define their own working answer to what co-creation means before they begin their project.

modules.title: The Prompt Is Your Paintbrush
modules.sub: Learning to direct AI output through deliberate, crafted prompting
modules.description: Learners experiment hands-on with prompting techniques, discovering how small changes in wording produce dramatically different results. They practise shaping tone, style, and content through iteration rather than accepting first outputs. This module builds the core skill of treating prompting as a creative act, not a lucky guess.

modules.title: Whose Idea Is It Anyway
modules.sub: Untangling authorship, credit, and creative ownership with AI
modules.description: Learners dig into the messy question of who owns an AI-assisted creation and what authorship even means in that context. They analyse case studies where credit is disputed and decide for themselves where the line sits between tool use and genuine creative contribution. They begin documenting their own decision-making process so they can defend their choices later.

modules.title: Remixing Without Ripping Off
modules.sub: Navigating originality, inspiration, and ethical creative borrowing
modules.description: Learners explore how AI models are trained on existing work and what that means for originality in their own projects. They distinguish between inspiration, remix, and plagiarism, and apply those distinctions to AI-generated content. By the end, they set personal ethical boundaries for how they will use AI sources in their own portfolio piece.

modules.title: Build Your AI-Assisted Portfolio Piece
modules.sub: Making a real creative project using AI as a deliberate collaborator
modules.description: Learners execute their own creative project — a piece of writing, a designed artefact, a short film, or another format — using an AI tool as an active collaborator. They make intentional choices about when to follow the AI's suggestions and when to override or redirect them, logging those decisions throughout. The result is a finished piece that reflects both the AI's contribution and their own distinct creative voice.

modules.title: Present and Defend Your Creation
modules.sub: Articulating your creative choices and owning your finished work
modules.description: Learners present their completed portfolio piece to a real or simulated audience and explain the creative decisions they made at every stage. They walk through specific moments where they took back control from the AI and explain why those choices define the work as theirs. This module turns the project into a rehearsed, confident argument for their own creative authorship.

synopsis: You've been given one week, one AI tool, and one brief â create something that matters to you. This course drops you into a real creative challenge where every decision about how to use AI shapes the final result, and teaches you when to trust the machine and when to take back control.
rationale: Teenagers are already using generative AI tools for creative work but lack structured frameworks for thinking about authorship, quality control, and ethical use, leaving them vulnerable to both poor outputs and academic integrity issues.
learning_outcome: Students can plan, execute, and critically evaluate a creative project made in collaboration with an AI tool, articulating which decisions they made themselves and why those choices define the work as their own.
